Mathematical Foundations of AI & ML
Unit 6: Statistical Inference and Estimation

Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz

FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg

01. Title: Statistical Inference and Estimation

  • Unit scope and motivation.

02. Learning outcomes

  • Estimation and uncertainty toolkit.

03. Recap from Unit 5

  • RVs, expectation, variance links.

04. Population vs sample

  • Inference objective.

05. Parameter vs statistic

  • Unknown truth vs estimator.

06. Point estimation overview

  • Single-value summaries.

07. Bias and unbiasedness

  • Systematic error notion.

08. Variance of estimators

  • Precision notion.

09. MSE decomposition

  • Bias-variance tradeoff.

10. Consistency concept

  • Behavior as n grows.

11. Efficiency intuition

  • Best estimator criteria.

12. Method of moments

  • Matching sample moments.

13. Maximum likelihood estimation

  • Likelihood maximization principle.

14. Log-likelihood convenience

  • Numerical stability benefits.

15. MLE examples scaffold

  • Bernoulli/Gaussian cases.

16. Regularized estimation preview

  • Penalized likelihood link.

17. Confidence intervals idea

  • Range of plausible parameters.

18. CI interpretation pitfalls

  • Frequentist meaning clarity.

19. Standard error concept

  • Sampling variability.

20. CLT-based intervals

  • Approximation conditions.

21. Bootstrap intuition

  • Resampling-based uncertainty.

22. Parametric vs nonparametric bootstrap

  • Assumption levels.

23. Hypothesis testing motivation

  • Decision under uncertainty.

24. Null and alternative hypotheses

  • Formal setup.

25. Test statistics

  • Evidence quantification.

26. p-value interpretation

  • What it is and is not.

27. Significance level alpha

  • Type I control.

28. Type I and Type II errors

  • Tradeoff structure.

29. Statistical power

  • Detectability concept.

30. Effect size vs significance

  • Practical relevance.

31. Multiple testing issue

  • False discovery inflation.

32. Correction methods (conceptual)

  • Bonferroni/FDR preview.

33. Model comparison framing

  • Nested vs non-nested hints.

34. Train/val/test vs hypothesis tests

  • Distinct roles in ML.

35. Data snooping and p-hacking risk

  • Reproducibility threat.

36. Pre-registration mindset (light)

  • Guard against bias.

37. Inference under shift

  • When assumptions break.

38. Robust estimation hints

  • Outlier-resilient alternatives.

39. Uncertainty communication

  • Intervals, bars, caveats.

40. Calibration bridge

  • Statistical reliability in ML.

41. Materials example: property mean CI

  • Practical lab interpretation.

42. Materials example: model comparison test

  • Baseline vs improved model.

43. Materials example: batch effects

  • Confounded inference.

44. Checklist for valid inference

  • Assumptions and diagnostics.

45. Formula summary

  • Exam-oriented scaffold.
